answer:
**The Vishnu Smṛti (work slug: unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8) does not contain a verse that enumerates the proper times for ritual bathing (e.g., a list such as dawn/noon/dusk or similar).**[^1]

Chapters 59–64 cover daily duties for householders (*gṛhastha*), including rising times, purification (*śauca*), sipping water (*ācamana*), chewing a tooth-stick (*dantadhāvana*), and bathing (*snāna*). Bathing is emphasized as daily, primarily in the morning after Brahma-muhūrta (pre-dawn), with detailed prohibitions:[^2]
- Not at night (except eclipses), not at twilight, not when ill or digesting.[^3]
- Morning bath: "after seeing the eastern sky become reddish-tawny."[^4]

Purification bathing occurs in many contexts (e.g., ch. 22–23 for death/impurity), but no verse lists "proper times" as a set (e.g., thrice daily). Other works like *Vratisaṃsāna* enumerate six ritual baths (*Āgneya*, *Vāruṇa*, etc.),[^5] but not this text.

[^1]: Confirmed via `search_corpus` ("ritual bathing", "bathing"/"snāna", times + bath), `read_wiki`/`read_chapter` (ch. 22–23, 59–64), `list_chapters`.
[^2]: [Chapter 60 (/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/60/translation/)](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/60/translation/), Brahma-muhūrta rise + *śauca* (Vi_60.1,24–26).
[^3]: [Chapter 64 (/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/64/translation/)](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/64/translation/), Vi_64.3–8.
[^4]: [Chapter 64, p-68d1c0 (/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/64/translation/#p-68d1c0)](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/64/translation/#p-68d1c0), Vi_64.9; daily bathing praised (Vi_64.35,42).
[^5]: *Vratisaṃsāna* ch. 1, [p-4502aa](/works/unknown-vratisasana-d0fe75/01/translation/#p-4502aa).
